title: portulaca pilosa var. simpliciuscula
intro: Portulaca pilosa var. simpliciuscula, also known as the small-flowered purslane or purslane-leaved portulaca, is a perennial herb belonging to the purslane family (Portulacaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Portulaca pilosa var. simpliciuscula include reducing fever, treating wounds and burns, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often made into infusions or decoctions, and its roots and rhizomes are used to create tinctures and ointments.
